name: Alto de Peña Angulo y Puerto de Orduña — circular desde Amurrio
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Amurrio traverse diverse landscapes within the Cantabrian Valleys of Álava, characterized by soft green mountains. The terrain features well-paved surfaces suitable for road biking, transitioning from oak and ash forests in the lower foothills to meadows and rocky areas at higher altitudes. This region offers a variety of options for road cyclists, from moderate rides through picturesque valleys to challenging ascents. Best road cycling routes around Amurrio The most popular road cycling route is Gordexola Valley - circular from Amurrio, a 39.9 miles (64.2 km) trail that takes 3 hours 6 minutes to complete, offering diverse landscapes with well-paved surfaces. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Alto de Peña Angulo y Puerto de Orduña — circular desde Amurrio, a difficult 48.7 miles (78.3 km) path. This route features challenging ascents and significant elevation gain, requiring very good fitness. Local road cyclists also love the Carretera A-3634 – Oxirando Tower loop from Amurrio, a 35.3 miles (56.9 km) trail leading through the Cantabrian Valleys of Álava, often completed in about 2 hours 45 minutes. Road cycling around Amurrio is defined by soft green mountains, oak and ash forests, and varied terrain. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to more demanding ascents. The routes in Amurrio are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 600 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Amurrio's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Amurrio?
A: Road cycling routes around Amurrio feature diverse landscapes within the Cantabrian Valleys of Álava. You'll find well-paved surfaces suitable for road biking, transitioning from oak and ash forests in the lower foothills to meadows and rocky areas at higher altitudes. The region is characterized by soft green mountains, offering a variety of gradients.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Amurrio?
A: Yes, Amurrio offers options for various ability levels. While specific beginner routes aren't highlighted, the region has moderate rides through picturesque valleys. Out of the 22 available routes, 3 are rated as easy, providing a good starting point for less experienced riders.

Q: What are some challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders in Amurrio?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, Amurrio offers several demanding routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1032737408/alto-de-pena-angulo-y-puerto-de-orduna-circular-desde-amurrio" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de Peña Angulo y Puerto de Orduña — circular desde Amurrio&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 78.3 km path with significant elevation gain. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31934073" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de Humaran – La Herbosa Viewpoint loop from Salbio&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 100 km and features substantial ascents.

Q: What natural attractions can I see while road cycling around Amurrio?
A: While road cycling, you can experience the stunning natural environment of Amurrio. The region is home to the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2824175" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nervión Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, the highest waterfall on the Iberian Peninsula, especially spectacular during rainy seasons. You might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5556651" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Delika Canyon&lt;/a&gt;, which feeds the waterfall, and the dramatic landscapes of the Sierra Salvada mountain range.

Q: Are there any scenic viewpoints along the road cycling routes in Amurrio?
A: Yes, the varied terrain around Amurrio offers several scenic viewpoints. You can find highlights such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6756359" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trees above the abyss&lt;/a&gt; and other general &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6593787" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;viewpoints&lt;/a&gt; that provide panoramic vistas of the Cantabrian Valleys and surrounding mountains. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3079407" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of Pico del Fraile&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4136536" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorbea Summit&lt;/a&gt; are also notable points of interest in the wider area.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available in Amurrio?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes in Amurrio are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Popular circular routes include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1032720507/gordexola-valley-circular-from-amurrio" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gordexola Valley - circular from Amurrio&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39542630" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carretera A-3634 – Oxirando Tower loop from Amurrio&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: When is the best time of year for road cycling in Amurrio?
A: Amurrio attracts tourists throughout the year due to its natural environment. For road cycling, the best time is generally during the milder seasons, such as spring and autumn, when temperatures are pleasant and the landscapes are vibrant. The impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2824175" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nervión Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; is particularly unique during the rainy season or thaw, which might influence a visit.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Amurrio?
A: The road cycling routes in Amurrio are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 10 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, well-paved surfaces, and the variety of options catering to different fitness levels, from moderate rides through picturesque valleys to challenging ascents.

Q: Can I find long-distance road cycling routes in Amurrio?
A: Yes, Amurrio offers several long-distance road cycling routes for those looking for extended rides. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1032737408/alto-de-pena-angulo-y-puerto-de-orduna-circular-desde-amurrio" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de Peña Angulo y Puerto de Orduña — circular desde Amurrio&lt;/a&gt; covers 78.3 km, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31934073" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de Humaran – La Herbosa Viewpoint loop from Salbio&lt;/a&gt; extends over 100 km, providing ample distance for endurance cyclists.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks near the road cycling routes?
A: While the focus is on natural beauty, Amurrio also has a rich architectural and cultural heritage. You might encounter features like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3342342" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fountain in Orduña&lt;/a&gt; or other local points of interest that reflect the region's history as you cycle through the towns and villages.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Amurrio?
A: There are over 20 road cycling routes available around Amurrio, offering a wide range of options for cyclists. These routes vary in difficulty, from easy to difficult, ensuring there's something for every skill level.
tips: Añes is located on an eminence at the bottom of the Llanteno valley and at the foot of the Sierra Salvada, which is crowned by a vertical rock wall. The landscape is of oak and ash groves, in the first foothills of the sierra, and of meadows and rocks as you gain altitude.
